semantic.role: Risk Hotspots Report – identifies files with the highest risk factors in the codebase, providing a ranked list and detailed analysis to guide maintenance priorities.
responsibilities: Presents a table of top risk files, each with rank, file name, risk factors, impact, and risk score.
Provides detailed analysis for each hotspot, explaining why it is a risk and what the impact is.
Acts as an experimental view generated and refreshed via `spine sync` command.
Helps teams focus refactoring and testing efforts on the most critical parts of the codebase.
outOfScope: Does not prescribe specific fixes or remediation steps.
Does not replace architectural reviews or manual code inspection.
Does not cover security vulnerabilities, performance benchmarks, or functional correctness.

English.purpose: This document surfaces risk hotspots in the project by analyzing code complexity, coupling, change frequency, and other risk factors. It helps developers and architects quickly identify which files pose the highest maintenance or quality risk so they can prioritize improvements.
English.context_and_audience: The report is generated by ArchSpine and is intended for all team members who need visibility into code health – including developers, lead developers, architects, and technical project managers. It is part of the views provided by ArchSpine and can be refreshed on demand via `spine sync`. This report is especially useful during code review planning, refactoring sprints, or technical debt assessments.
key_takeaways: Files are ranked by risk score, combining various risk factors such as cyclomatic complexity, churn, and dependency count.
The detailed analysis section explains the specific risk drivers for each file.
The report is experimental and can be regenerated to reflect the latest state of the codebase.
Use this report to direct quality assurance and refactoring efforts to the most impactful areas.
